questions.question: Explain the SYSGEN (System Generation) process. What information does the SYSGEN program need?
keyPoints: SYSGEN is the process of configuring an OS for specific hardware
CPU information: type, number of cores, features
Memory information: amount of RAM, configuration
Device information: types of devices, addresses, IRQs, DMA channels
OS options: scheduling algorithm, file system type, max processes
Three approaches: recompile kernel, table-driven, modular/LKM

questions.question: Compare the three approaches to operating system generation.
keyPoints: Recompile kernel: modify source and compile, most efficient but inflexible
Table-driven: pre-compiled modules selected by configuration table, no compilation needed
Modular/LKM: small core with loadable modules, most flexible, used by modern Linux
Each approach has tradeoffs in flexibility, size, and performance

questions.question: Describe the Linux kernel compilation process.
keyPoints: make modules to compile loadable modules
make install to install kernel to /boot
